title: 清洁女工之死
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\209-清洁女工之死-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
James Bentley discusses local residents with Hercule Poirot, recalling comments made by Mrs. MacKenzie. Bentley mentions Mr. Carpenter’s new house and engagement to Mrs. Sherlock, and notes Mrs. MacKenzie's disdain for her. He also recalls Mrs. MacKenzie’s negative views of the Weatherby couple, describing the wife as a "fuss" maker and the husband as silent, noting it was an unhappy family. Poirot observes that Bentley is deeply engaged in thought regarding life at Hunter's Manor. Bentley reveals he helped a Silky Terrier caught in a trap and discusses his conversation with Dierdre Henderson. He mentions that Henderson's mother suffered and that her stepfather was involved in her difficulties. Bentley defends Henderson's attractiveness, causing him to blush.

In another setting, Mrs. Sweetman pressures Edna to report what she witnessed regarding the murder of Mrs. Upward. Edna, distraught and fearful of her father's reaction, refuses to speak to Bert Helling or a "foreign gentleman." Mrs. Weatherby enters the post office to buy knitting supplies and discusses her recent ill health and her dissatisfaction with her help's manners. She also comments on the local atmosphere of fear following the murder, criticizing Mrs. Samohes of the Long Grass Inn for not locking her doors. Mrs. Sweetman informs Mrs. Weatherby that Mr. Upward is staying at the Long Grass Inn due to his distress, while Janet Groom has returned to her hometown. 

Colonel Johnny Samoyski enters the post office to mail packages. Mrs. Sweetman asks for his advice regarding Edna, explaining that Edna saw something on the night of the murder. She asks the Colonel to confirm a rumor that a lady was having coffee with Mrs. Upward on the night of the crime.
